meta.title: Washoe County & Reno — June 9, 2026 Primary Voter Guide
meta.disclaimer: Independent, non-endorsing guide. Every position links to its source. Where no sourced stance was found, the cell is left blank rather than guessed. Verify your specific ballot at washoecounty.gov/voters.
meta.primaryRules: Nevada runs a CLOSED partisan primary: registered Republicans and Democrats vote only their own party in partisan races (Governor, Congress, Legislature, partisan county offices). NONPARTISAN races (Reno/Sparks mayor & council, school board, judges) appear for EVERY voter, including nonpartisan/independent registrants.

budget.label: Budget & Fiscal
budget.desc: Municipal/county/state fiscal health, deficits, spending priorities.

dataCenters.label: Data Centers
dataCenters.desc: Data-center development, water/power use, tax abatements.

economy.label: Economy & Jobs
economy.desc: Jobs, cost of living, economic development.

races.title: U.S. House — Nevada District 2
races.notes: Open seat. Rep. Mark Amodei (R) departed in Feb. 2026, leaving Nevada's only safely Republican U.S. House seat open for the first time since 2011. CLOSED primary on June 9, 2026: only registered Republicans may vote in the GOP primary and only registered Democrats in the Democratic primary. 13 Republicans and 11 Democrats filed; the winners advance to the Nov. 2026 general. Three Democrats (Hebert, White, Jolle) and one Republican (Watts) suspended/withdrew their campaigns but remain on the ballot. GOP front-runners: James Settelmeyer (establishment-backed) and David Flippo (Trump-endorsed). Democratic front-runners: Teresa Benitez-Thompson and Greg Kidd.

races.title: Nevada Attorney General
races.notes: OPEN seat (incumbent Aaron Ford term-limited, running for governor). CLOSED primaries, both contested. Democratic: Senate Majority Leader Nicole Cannizzaro vs. State Treasurer Zach Conine. Republican: Adriana Guzman Fralick (endorsed by Trump and Gov. Lombardo) vs. Douglas County Commissioner Danny Tarkanian.

races.title: Nevada Governor
races.notes: CLOSED primary (June 9, 2026): only registered party members vote in their party's contest. Top vote-getter in each party advances to Nov. 3, 2026 general. Republican primary: incumbent Gov. Joe Lombardo vs. several longshot challengers. Democratic primary: term-limited AG Aaron Ford (frontrunner) vs. Washoe County Commission Chair Alexis Hill, plus minor candidates. Rated a competitive/leans-Republican race nationally.

races.title: Nevada Secretary of State
races.notes: Nevada's chief elections officer. CLOSED primaries. Incumbent Democrat Cisco Aguilar advanced to the general without a primary opponent (no other Democrat filed). Contested four-way Republican primary: former Assemblyman/2020 'fake elector' Jim Marchant, former Assemblywoman Sharron Angle, attorney/developer Shirley Folkins-Roberts (Lombardo-endorsed), and Socorro Keenan. Election-integrity claims are central to the GOP race.

races.title: Nevada Lieutenant Governor
races.notes: CLOSED primary. Incumbent Republican Lt. Gov. Stavros Anthony is unopposed in the GOP primary. Contested Democratic primary: Assembly Majority Floor Leader Sandra Jauregui (frontrunner), pediatric ICU nurse Courtney Burke, and performer Bridget 'BridgieNix' Scheiner. Office is the first-in-line to the governorship.

races.title: Nevada State Controller
races.notes: State's chief fiscal officer. CLOSED primaries. Incumbent Republican Andy Matthews is unopposed in the GOP primary. Contested Democratic primary: Robert Blackstock, Henderson high-school teacher Michael MacDougall, and Robert Tolle.

races.title: Nevada State Treasurer
races.notes: OPEN seat (incumbent Zach Conine running for AG). CLOSED primaries, both contested. Democratic: Assemblymember Joe Dalia, deputy state treasurer Tya Mathis-Coleman, educator Jay Maharjan. Republican: budget watchdog Drew Johnson and former Chicago Mercantile Exchange trader Jeff Carter.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 24 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Democratic incumbent Erica Roth advanced unopposed; her November opponent is Andrew Schaefer (No Political Party). Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 25
races.notes: CLOSED Republican primary on June 9, 2026. AD25 covers big sections of Reno around Meadowood Mall, Caughlin Ranch, and northwest Reno (Mae Anne Ave / Robb Dr). Michael Ginsburg vs. Noah Bachman Dodge; no incumbent in the GOP race. Winner challenges Democratic incumbent Selena La Rue Hatch in November. District leans Democratic by roughly 2 points. Key issues: housing affordability, education funding, public safety, EV/highway funding, tax abatements.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 26 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Both primaries were canceled: Democrat Sam Baugh and Republican incumbent Rich DeLong each advanced unopposed to the November general. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 27 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Democratic incumbent Heather Goulding and Republican Veronica Cortes each advanced unopposed; primaries canceled. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 30 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Democratic incumbent Natha Anderson and Republican Trista Gomez each advanced unopposed; primaries canceled. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 31 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Democrat Neal Schwartz and Republican incumbent Jill Dickman each advanced unopposed; primaries canceled. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 32 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Republican incumbent Alexis Hansen advanced unopposed; the Democratic primary was canceled. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Assembly — District 40
races.notes: CLOSED Republican primary on June 9, 2026 with four candidates: Julie Butler, Rich Harvey, Drew Ribar, and Stacy Woodbury. AD40 is centered on Carson City and Storey County but includes a piece of south Washoe County, so it appears for some Washoe voters. Open GOP race (incumbent P.K. O'Neill is running for state Senate). Winner faces Democrat Oscar Fuentes (unopposed in the Democratic primary) in November. Key issues: affordability/cost of living, water, wildfire, energy/utility costs, regulatory and government-spending reform, public safety, immigration.

races.title: Nevada State Senate — District 13 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT on the June 2026 primary ballot as a contested race. Democratic incumbent Richard 'Skip' Daly, Republican Matthew R. Buehler, and Independent American Charlene Young each advanced unopposed within their parties; the general-election matchup is set for November. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Senate — District 14 (no contested primary)
races.notes: NOT contested. Republican incumbent Ira Hansen drew no primary challenger; the Democratic primary was canceled. Listed for completeness only.

races.title: Nevada State Senate — District 16
races.notes: CLOSED Republican primary on June 9, 2026: only registered Republicans vote. Incumbent Lisa Krasner (R) faces challenger Monica Jaye Stabbert. District 16 spans south Reno/Washoe and the Carson City area. Winner faces Democrat Chrystal Roll (unopposed in the Democratic primary) in November. Only Washoe-area state Senate seat with a contested primary in 2026. Key issues: wildfire, education, election integrity, cost of living/housing, public safety, water/growth from Northern Nevada's data-center and manufacturing boom.

races.title: Washoe County Commission, District 2
races.notes: Covers south Reno and Washoe Valley. Contested Republican primary (Clark vs. Killoran); CLOSED to registered Republicans only. Democrat Marie Rodriguez awaits the winner in November.

races.title: Washoe County Commission, District 5
races.notes: Open seat (Jeanne Herman term-limited). Sprawling ~5,000 sq mi rural district. Three-way Republican primary (Holt-Still, Pierce, Yriarte); CLOSED to Republicans only. Democrat Brian Wadsworth and Libertarian Lisa Lee await in November.

races.title: Washoe County District Attorney
races.notes: Winner-take-all CLOSED Republican primary (Hicks vs. Duncan) under a 2015 Nevada law: with no Democrat filed, the GOP primary winner effectively wins the office outright on June 9.

races.title: Washoe County Sheriff
races.notes: Sheriff is now a nonpartisan office in Nevada. Incumbent Darin Balaam is unopposed in 2026. Open to ALL voters.

races.title: Washoe County Assessor
races.notes: Incumbent Chris Sarman (R) is unopposed.

races.title: Washoe County Clerk
races.notes: Up for election in 2026. Incumbent Jan Galassini (R). No challenger identified in available sources; appears uncontested.

races.title: Washoe County Public Administrator
races.notes: Incumbent Nicole Klitzke (R) endorsed by Washoe GOP; no challenger identified in available sources.

races.title: Washoe County Recorder
races.notes: Up for election in 2026. Incumbent Kalie Work (D). No challenger identified in available sources; appears uncontested.

races.title: Washoe County Treasurer
races.notes: Incumbent Justin Taylor (R) is unopposed.

races.title: WCSD Board of Trustees, District B
races.notes: Incumbent Colleen Westlake is unopposed.

races.title: WCSD Board of Trustees, District C
races.notes: Incumbent James 'JJ' Phoenix is unopposed.

races.title: WCSD Board of Trustees, District F (At-Large)
races.notes: At-large seat opened by Adam Mayberry leaving to run for Sparks City Council. District F covers Sparks, central Reno, and south Reno east of I-580. Three candidates; top two advance to November. Only competitive WCSD board race in 2026 (incumbents Colleen Westlake and James Phoenix unopposed).

races.title: Reno City Council Ward 2
races.notes: Open seat — incumbent Naomi Duerr is termed out. 4 candidates in the June 9, 2026 primary; top two advance to November. Redrawn Ward 2 covers west Reno from West Fourth Street to Lakeridge Golf Course. Nonpartisan, open to ALL voters.

races.title: Reno City Council Ward 4
races.notes: Only 2 candidates filed (incumbent Meghan Ebert and Eddie/Eduardo Gomez), so there is NO June primary for this seat — both advance directly to the Nov. 3, 2026 general. Ward 4 covers the North Valleys and the largest area zoned for industrial/data center development. Included here for completeness.

races.title: Reno Mayor
races.notes: Open seat — incumbent Mayor Hillary Schieve is termed out. 9 candidates on the June 9, 2026 primary ballot; top two advance to the Nov. 3, 2026 general. Nonpartisan, open to ALL voters. Dominant issues: structural budget deficit (~$24-26M) and a hot-button data center moratorium.

races.title: Sparks Mayor
races.notes: Incumbent Ed Lawson faces three challengers amid city fiscal concerns. Nonpartisan; open to all Sparks voters. If no candidate exceeds 50% in the June 9 primary, top two advance to the November general.

races.title: Sparks City Council Ward 2
races.notes: Four candidates including appointed/elected incumbent Dian VanderWell. Nonpartisan; if no candidate exceeds 50% in the primary, top two advance to November.

races.title: Sparks City Council Ward 4
races.notes: Open seat; incumbent Charlene Bybee is term-limited and running for mayor. Four candidates. Nonpartisan; top two advance to November if no majority.

candidates.name: James Settelmeyer
candidates.bio: Fourth-generation Gardnerville-area rancher and former Nevada state Senate Republican leader (16 years in the Legislature) who resigned as director of the Nevada Department of Conservation and Natural Resources to run. Endorsed by Gov. Joe Lombardo and former Rep. Mark Amodei.

sources.label: Nevada Independent — Settelmeyer driver's license vote fact-check

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet James Settelmeyer, GOP candidate for Nevada Congressional District 2

taxes.stance: Limited government and low taxes; touts suing and beating Democratic leaders over a tax hike passed without the required two-thirds vote.
taxes.quote: the courts refunded it after they determined at the United Nevada Supreme Court that the governor and the Democratic Party violated the Nevada Constitution, unanimously.
taxes.videoQuote: the courts refunded it after they determined at the United Nevada Supreme Court that the governor and the Democratic Party violated the Nevada Constitution, unanimously.

immigration.stance: Supports border security, especially for those who committed crimes beyond illegal entry; backs a path forward for people brought to the U.S. as children.

abortion.stance: Says abortion and red flag gun laws should be left to state-level decisions.

economy.stance: Focuses on rural Nevada agriculture; wants to address meat-packing industry consolidation (notes four firms control ~85% of the market).
economy.quote: you have four people that literally control 85% of the industry. That's not supposed to be allowed. That's called a monopoly.
economy.videoQuote: you have four people that literally control 85% of the industry. That's not supposed to be allowed. That's called a monopoly.

water.stance: Pledges to protect Nevada's water resources in Congress.
water.quote: In Congress, he will fight to secure our borders, strengthen our economy, protect Nevada's water resources, and preserve the freedoms that make Nevada and America exceptional.
water.videoQuote: I am very well versed in the natural resources such as water law, which you know, nobody argues about water in state of Nevada at all…

housing.stance: Supports lands-bill development next to existing growth but warns high per-acre land prices undermine affordable, attainable housing.
housing.quote: We need affordable attainable housing. And if you have to buy the land at that price, you're not going to have affordable attainable housing.

candidates.summary: Establishment-backed rancher and ex-state Senate GOP leader; runs on low taxes, water rights, and border security.

candidates.name: David Flippo
candidates.bio: Retired Air Force Lt. Col. (Iraq veteran), small-business owner and nonprofit leader who moved to Nevada about a decade ago. Placed second in the 2024 CD4 GOP primary before switching to CD2; endorsed by President Trump and the Washoe County GOP. Largely self-funding.

sources.label: Nevada Current — Trump endorses Flippo; Amodei backs Settelmeyer

immigration.stance: Centers campaign on illegal immigration and border security; cites a Battle Mountain victim of an undocumented immigrant who had a driver's license.
immigration.quote: Tragedies like this should never happen in America.

economy.stance: Wants Trump executive orders codified into law; ties housing supply problems to undocumented immigration.

candidates.summary: Trump-endorsed retired Air Force colonel making illegal immigration and border security his central issue.

candidates.name: Fred Simon Jr.
candidates.bio: Trauma surgeon and small-business owner from Gardnerville, age 73. Previously ran for Congress in California, for Nevada governor in 2022, and for this seat in 2024, where he won about 33% of the GOP primary vote.

economy.stance: Emphasizes balancing the budget and a comprehensive energy and immigration policy; would combat corporate price gouging where profits rise despite higher prices.

candidates.summary: Gardnerville trauma surgeon and perennial candidate stressing a balanced budget and energy/immigration policy.

candidates.name: Tom Doyle
candidates.bio: Reno resident with 45 years in the casino industry and inactive CPA credentials; cites experience with the Nevada Gaming Control Board.

immigration.stance: Supports DACA to a degree but opposes birthright citizenship; says criminal undocumented immigrants should be prioritized for deportation.
immigration.quote: Criminal illegal immigrants should be prioritized for deportation

taxes.stance: Opposes additional taxation of higher earners; would alter rather than eliminate the Affordable Care Act.

economy.stance: Would broadly continue Amodei's voting record; expects gas prices to fall as Iran conflict resolves.
economy.quote: would probably support almost everything Mark Amodei has voted for

candidates.summary: 45-year casino-industry veteran who would largely continue Amodei's record; backs DACA but opposes birthright citizenship.

candidates.name: George Forbush
candidates.bio: Retired police officer and fourth-generation Nevadan, age 52, who worked for the Humboldt County Sheriff's Office and Sparks Police Department.

housing.stance: Proposes 50-year mortgages to improve housing affordability.

immigration.stance: Backs a merit-based immigration points system.

economy.stance: Advocates a new fuel pipeline from Utah and regulating pharmaceutical and insurance industries to limit price gouging.

candidates.summary: Retired police officer pitching 50-year mortgages, merit-based immigration, and curbs on drug-industry price gouging.

candidates.name: Jerry Olsen
candidates.bio: Retired Army medic and former Washoe County deputy sheriff (20 years), age 65, running a constituent-first campaign and not accepting donations.

transparency.stance: Pledges to vote the way constituents want rather than his own preference; says he is not '100 percent with Trump.'
transparency.quote: I'm going to vote what the constituents want, I'm not going to vote the way I want

taxes.stance: Would eliminate taxes for people over age 70.

candidates.summary: Retired Army medic and ex-deputy taking no donations; pledges to vote his constituents' will, not Trump's line.

candidates.name: Mike Smith
candidates.bio: Retired Navy captain and fighter pilot from Sparks, age 69, who served as Nevada's U.S. Navy emergency preparedness liaison officer.

budget.stance: Names the national debt as his top priority.

economy.stance: Would get rid of the Affordable Care Act; criticized the Washoe County GOP's endorsement of Flippo as disenfranchising voters.

candidates.summary: Retired Navy fighter pilot whose top priority is the national debt; would repeal the ACA.

candidates.name: Sherman Tylawsky
candidates.bio: Carson City resident, age 28, a Ph.D. candidate and founder of The George Washington Institute who previously interned in Congress.

taxes.stance: Questions the value of income taxes versus consumption taxes; wants fewer regulations and taxes.

immigration.stance: Supports Trump's immigration rhetoric and enforcement actions.

economy.stance: Wants to grow the supply chain to address affordability of gas, groceries and water.

candidates.summary: 28-year-old Ph.D. candidate and think-tank founder favoring consumption taxes and fewer regulations.

candidates.name: Jesse Watts
candidates.bio: Former Eureka County sheriff from Golden Valley, age 40, and executive director of the nonprofit Wounded Blue. Suspended his campaign to endorse Settelmeyer after Trump endorsed Flippo, but his name remains on the ballot.

economy.stance: Would build a Nevada oil refinery via public-private partnership (~$30B) and add alternative pipelines from Utah/Texas; supports deregulation and infrastructure.

taxes.stance: Would eliminate senior taxes and repeal the ACA; supports codifying DOGE; opposes Ukraine war funding.

candidates.summary: Ex-Eureka County sheriff who suspended his campaign for Settelmeyer; pitched a Nevada oil refinery and deregulation.

candidates.name: Jennifer Billat
candidates.bio: Reno-area land development business owner, age 45, running primarily on public-lands issues and aligned with Amodei's land and environmental protection work.

economy.stance: Wants fuel independence from California and housing expansion; makes public land her signature issue.
economy.quote: We're getting gas from California right now, that's just a bad plan

candidates.summary: Land-development owner running primarily on public-lands issues and fuel independence from California.

candidates.name: Andrea Lowe
candidates.bio: Homemaker and eight-year volunteer firefighter from Austin, age 32, a seventh-generation Nevadan who serves on the Lander County School Board.

economy.stance: Names healthcare as her top priority and emphasizes bringing jobs to Nevada and younger leadership.

candidates.summary: 32-year-old volunteer firefighter and school board member running on healthcare, jobs, and younger leadership.

candidates.name: Bruce Grego
candidates.bio: Attorney and former South Lake Tahoe city councilman, age 73, who moved to Carson City in 2023.

transparency.stance: Campaigns on taking on the 'administrative state' and unelected officials.
transparency.quote: I don't think unelected officials should be controlling our lives

candidates.summary: Attorney and ex-South Lake Tahoe councilman campaigning against the 'administrative state' and unelected officials.

candidates.name: Rick Shepherd
candidates.bio: Reno resident, age 54, who was a Bernie Sanders delegate and ran as a Democrat in 2020 before registering Republican; holds heterodox positions for a GOP primary.

economy.stance: Supports universal healthcare and free public services; proposes desalinating brackish water via reverse osmosis and molten-salt reactors to cut electricity costs.

abortion.stance: Supports abortion rights and opposes Trump's immigration enforcement.

electionIntegrity.stance: Argues U.S. elections have not been free and fair.
electionIntegrity.quote: We haven't had a free, fair election in a long time

candidates.summary: Former Bernie delegate running in the GOP primary; backs universal healthcare, abortion rights, and election skepticism.

candidates.name: Teresa Benitez-Thompson
candidates.bio: Former Nevada Assembly majority leader (first elected 2010) and licensed social worker who served as chief of staff to Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford. The most experienced and best-known Democrat in the field.

economy.stance: Makes cost of living her top focus; vows to restore federal funds she says DOGE stripped from Nevada and to build affordable workforce housing.
economy.quote: addressing costs for families will be my number one job

immigration.stance: Calls for a reformed immigration system and an end to aggressive enforcement she says destabilizes families and the job market.
immigration.quote: We need a reformed immigration system that works, not federal agents pulling families apart.

abortion.stance: Supports protecting reproductive freedom through federal legislation.
abortion.quote: Healthcare should be as accessible and inexpensive as possible

water.stance: Opposes large-scale public land sales without local and tribal collaboration and wants federal wildfire prevention funding restored.

candidates.summary: Former Assembly majority leader and social worker; the field's most experienced Democrat, focused on cost of living.

candidates.name: Robert "Greg" Kidd
candidates.bio: Wealthy venture investor and founder of the Hard Yaka venture firm with prior Federal Reserve experience. A self-funder who won ~36% as a nonpartisan candidate in 2024 before running as a Democrat in 2026; considered Benitez-Thompson's chief rival.

economy.stance: Names affordability — groceries, gas, housing, healthcare — as the top issue and runs on a record of fighting Big Tech and big banks.
economy.quote: Affordability. Everybody knows that, whether it's grocery prices, gas, housing and in particular, health care.

dataCenters.stance: Says subsidizing the data-center boom makes no sense given water and power shortages; data centers should pay the state and return power/water.
dataCenters.quote: I do think... a moratorium is not enough

candidates.summary: Self-funding venture investor and Benitez-Thompson's chief rival; runs on affordability and reining in data centers.

candidates.name: Kathy Durham
candidates.bio: Union representative and high school civics teacher from rural Nevada who comes from a military veteran family.

economy.stance: Supports Medicare for All and opposes corporate consolidation.

candidates.summary: Union rep and civics teacher backing Medicare for All and opposing corporate consolidation.

candidates.name: Matthew Fonken
candidates.bio: Northern Nevada organizer and former Nevada Democratic Party executive director who has worked in hospitality and as a state Assembly attaché.

transparency.stance: Supports congressional term limits, a 5-year lobbying ban, and a ban on stock trading by members of Congress.

economy.stance: Supports Medicare for All.

candidates.summary: Ex-state Democratic Party director pushing term limits, a lobbying ban, and a congressional stock-trading ban.

candidates.name: Morgan Wadsworth
candidates.bio: Student and former casino worker, age 27, a sixth-generation Nevadan from a conservative ranching family who became the consensus pick of the 'anti-establishment' Democratic coalition after Hebert, White and Jolle endorsed her.

economy.stance: Supports Medicare for All.

housing.stance: Wants to rezone vacant lots to make housing more affordable.
housing.quote: rezoning vacant lots to make housing more affordable

immigration.stance: Calls for abolishing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).

dataCenters.stance: Opposes building data centers due to water pollution risk.

candidates.summary: 27-year-old anti-establishment consensus pick; backs Medicare for All, abolishing ICE, and opposing data centers.

candidates.name: Gamy Enriquez
candidates.bio: Gamaliel 'Gamy' Zavala Enriquez, a DoorDash driver and small-business owner, grandson of a bracero laborer, who has experienced homelessness and incarceration and holds a master's in public administration.

economy.stance: Proposes a $1,300 monthly universal basic income funded by Nevada's mineral resources, plus a 'Green Marshall Plan' and universal child care/parental leave.
economy.quote: $1,300 monthly universal basic income funded by Nevada's mineral resources

candidates.summary: DoorDash driver who has faced homelessness; proposes a $1,300 UBI funded by Nevada minerals and a 'Green Marshall Plan.'

candidates.name: Johnny Kerns
candidates.bio: Retired homicide investigator and true-crime author who received about 2% in a 2024 state Senate primary.

budget.stance: Focuses on stabilizing the economy and the ~$39 trillion national debt; says society will adapt to AI's workplace effects.

candidates.summary: Retired homicide investigator and true-crime author focused on the economy and national debt.

candidates.name: Gerold Gorman
candidates.bio: Gerold Lee Gorman, a 2022 Nevada congressional candidate with minimal documented 2026 campaign activity.
candidates.summary: 2022 congressional candidate with minimal documented 2026 activity; no stated positions.

candidates.name: Josh Hebert
candidates.bio: Physics Ph.D. and self-described policy wonk who organized the 'anti-establishment' Democratic coalition. Withdrew in May 2026 and endorsed Morgan Wadsworth, but his name remains on the ballot.

transparency.stance: Signature issue is getting big money out of politics via a constitutional 'Ban Big Money Amendment' limiting political spending to voting-age citizens.
transparency.quote: The source of all political spending has to be voting age citizens. No corporations, no organizations, no super PACs, no unions, just people.

candidates.summary: Physics Ph.D. who organized the anti-establishment coalition; withdrew to back Wadsworth on a money-in-politics platform.

candidates.name: Mark Jolle
candidates.bio: Democratic candidate and member of the CD2 'anti-establishment' coalition who withdrew from the race in May 2026 without endorsing a candidate; his name remains on the ballot.
candidates.summary: Anti-establishment coalition Democrat who withdrew without endorsing; name remains on the ballot.

candidates.name: Samuel White
candidates.bio: Democratic candidate and member of the CD2 'anti-establishment' coalition who withdrew in May 2026 and endorsed Morgan Wadsworth; his name remains on the ballot.
candidates.summary: Anti-establishment coalition Democrat who withdrew and endorsed Wadsworth; name remains on the ballot.

candidates.name: Lisa Krasner
candidates.bio: Incumbent state senator for District 16, first elected to the Senate in 2022 after three terms in the Assembly (District 26, elected 2016). Attorney (Juris Doctor) and adjunct college instructor teaching the U.S. and Nevada Constitutions.

sources.label: Nevada Appeal — GOP candidates AD40/SD16 discuss the issues (May 16, 2026)
Sierra Nevada Ally — Who's Running in the Senate (2026)

education.stance: Names education one of Nevada's three greatest challenges and a priority for the next legislative session.

electionIntegrity.stance: Lists election integrity among the top challenges she wants to address in the next session.

publicSafety.stance: Frames wildfire prevention/response as a top-tier statewide challenge for the next session.

economy.stance: Says national/Republican policies should be embraced only where tailored to Nevada's specific needs, not one-size-fits-all.
economy.quote: The political policies that are appropriate for Nevadans must be tailored for the people of Nevada and the needs of Nevadans.

candidates.summary: Incumbent SD16 senator and attorney naming wildfire, election integrity, and education as Nevada's top challenges.

candidates.name: Monica Jaye Stabbert
candidates.bio: Conservative talk-radio host (The Monica Jaye Show) with 30+ years in broadcasting; small-business owner from a family automotive background. Vice President of the Nevada Humane Society and First VP of Mt. Rose Republican Women. Challenging incumbent Lisa Krasner in the SD16 GOP primary.

sources.label: Nevada Appeal — GOP candidates AD40/SD16 discuss the issues (May 16, 2026)
Sierra Nevada Ally — Who's Running in the Senate (2026)

housing.stance: Names cost of living and housing affordability her top challenge; wants to build on AB540, invest in middle-income attainable housing, and cut permitting red tape.
housing.quote: Cost of living and housing affordability. Families are being squeezed from every direction. I'd like to see the Legislature build on AB540, continue investing in attainable housing for middle-income families, cut permitting red tape and keep pushing back on new taxes and fees.

taxes.stance: Wants to keep pushing back on new taxes and fees as part of easing cost of living.
taxes.quote: keep pushing back on new taxes and fees

education.stance: Ties workforce development to schools: wants stronger career/technical education, more apprenticeships, and fewer licensing barriers so Nevadans fill local jobs.
education.quote: I want to see legislation that strengthens career and technical education in our high schools, expands apprenticeships and partnerships between employers and our community colleges and removes licensing barriers that keep qualified people out of good-paying trades.

dataCenters.stance: Points to Northern Nevada's data-center, Tesla, Switch and manufacturing boom and wants Nevadans, not imported labor, filling those jobs.
dataCenters.quote: Northern Nevada is exploding — Tesla, Switch, data centers, advanced manufacturing, logistics. The jobs are coming, but we have to make sure Nevadans are the ones filling them.

publicSafety.stance: Backs stronger penalties for violent and repeat offenders plus recruitment/retention support for law enforcement and first responders.
publicSafety.quote: I want to see stronger penalties for violent and repeat offenders, support for recruitment and retention and accountability for those who break our laws.

immigration.stance: Supports President Trump's focus on securing the border and enforcing immigration laws where it aligns with Nevada's interests.
immigration.quote: I support the President's focus on securing the border and enforcing our immigration laws, his push for energy independence and tax policies that let families and small businesses keep more of what they earn.

candidates.summary: Conservative talk-radio host challenging Krasner; centers cost of living, attainable housing, and local hiring.

candidates.name: Michael Ginsburg
candidates.bio: Third-generation Reno resident, age 63; manager of distribution design services at NV Energy/Sierra Pacific Power Co. Founding president of the Sierra Nevada Community Aquatics nonprofit (Moana pool project). Previously ran for state Senate District 15.

sources.label: Reno Gazette Journal candidate guide (via AOL) — Republicans Ginsburg, Bachman Dodge seek NV Assembly District 25

budget.stance: Would avoid cutting until studying the impacts; would protect public safety, health, and education as core state responsibilities.
budget.quote: My priorities, however, are public safety, health, and education. Those are the core responsibilities for the State. If cuts need to be made, I will protect the funding for those key areas.

taxes.stance: Skeptical of transferable tax credits for big projects; says abatements should stay with the intended project, citing past general-fund losses.
taxes.quote: I don't like that the credits are transferable — they should stay with the project for which they were intended.

housing.stance: Wants to address the affordable-housing crisis through zoning reform, streamlined permitting, and land acquisition.

growth.stance: Proposes a fee/method to capture lost highway-fund revenue from electric vehicles, which he says are heavier and strain infrastructure while paying little.
growth.quote: We need to make sure EVs are contributing to the highway fund, just like those of us who drive gas vehicles.

candidates.summary: Reno NV Energy manager wary of transferable tax credits; pushes EV highway fees and housing zoning reform.

candidates.name: Noah Bachman Dodge
